issue: merge(intervals) is supposed to combine closed integer intervals that overlap OR merely touch at a shared endpoint, since the endpoints are included in each interval. Right now intervals that only touch are left separate: merge([[1, 3], [3, 5]]) returns [[1, 3], [3, 5]] instead of the expected [[1, 5]]. Genuinely overlapping intervals and clearly-disjoint intervals are handled correctly; only the touching boundary is wrong. The overlaps() helper already treats touching intervals as overlapping, so merge() should be consistent with it.
